task_completion.description: Open the geoprocessing tools dialog in gvSIG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Geoprocessing' toolbox window or menu is open in the gvSIG interface.

task_completion.description: Select the tool to convert multipart to singlepart geometries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tool named 'Multipart to singlepart', 'Explode', or similar geometry conversion tool is selected or active in the geoprocessing dialog.

task_completion.description: Select the correct input layer for the geoprocessing tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' is visible in the input layer dropdown or text field of the active geoprocessing tool dialog.

task_completion.description: Set the correct export path for the resulting shapefile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/countries_singlepart.shp' is entered into the output file field of the geoprocessing dialog.

task_completion.description: Ensure the resulting singlepart layer is added to the View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new layer (e.g., 'countries_singlepart') appears in the Table of Contents (left panel) of the main gvSIG window, indicating it has been added to the View.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the gvSIG desktop application, specifically its menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are shown executing spatial commands like 'ogr2ogr' or Python scripts to explode the geometries.

integrity.description: The new layer was genuinely created via the geoprocessing tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows the geoprocessing dialog being configured and run before the new layer appears in the Table of Contents, rather than just renaming an existing layer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input layer: 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries'. Expected output path: '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/countries_singlepart.shp'. The original layer has 177 features. The exploded output will have exactly 289 features. If the agent opens the attribute table of the new layer, verify that the feature count is >200 (specifically 289). The task strictly requires using gvSIG's geoprocessing tools.
